title: China Economy - South China Morning Post
description: China economy news, including budget, the stock market and analysis.

items.title: China food-security risks worsen as extreme weather becomes more common
items.creator: Brian Rhoads,Raymond Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China food-security risks worsen as extreme weather becomes more common
items.content:
China’s Politburo called for “forceful” countermeasures against floods and droughts as El Nino effects exacerbate the impact of extreme weather events in the country.
Local authorities should strengthen flood controls on major rivers, improve rescue capabilities and bolster management of water sources, the top decision-making body said after a monthly meeting on Tuesday chaired by President Xi Jinping. Other steps include greater protection for agricultural infrastructure, according to...

items.title: Why the World Bank is winding down loans to China amid a ‘natural’ shift
items.creator: Sylvia Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Why the World Bank is winding down loans to China amid a ‘natural’ shift
items.content:
Beijing has downplayed reports that the World Bank will phase out lending to China by 2031, with the Ministry of Finance noting that the nation’s advancing economy and changing development needs were shifting cooperation away from financing and towards knowledge sharing.
“This is the natural result of changing domestic needs and the transformation of bilateral cooperation,” the ministry said in a statement on Wednesday, referring to the World Bank’s declining lending to China in recent...

items.title: European companies ‘becoming more reliant on China, not less’, EU chamber chief says
items.creator: Huizhao Huang
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: European companies ‘becoming more reliant on China, not less’, EU chamber chief says
items.content:
Europe’s push to reduce its dependence on China is driving many of its companies deeper into Chinese supply chains, according to the head of the European Union Chamber of Commerce in China.
“Europe is becoming more dependent on China, not less,” chamber president Jens Eskelund said, adding that for European firms, staying competitive increasingly meant embedding themselves more deeply in the supply chains Brussels wanted them to move away from.
He said Europe had fundamentally misread what China...

items.title: New technologies power China’s Tesla challengers to monthly sales records
items.creator: Daniel Ren
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: New technologies power China’s Tesla challengers to monthly sales records
items.content:
Chinese Tesla challengers Leapmotor and Zeekr, banking on their latest battery and self-driving technologies, bucked a downward trend in domestic electric vehicle sales with record deliveries last month, ratcheting up pressure on the US carmaker amid weak consumer sentiment towards big-ticket items.
Stellantis-backed Leapmotor delivered 93,376 electric vehicles (EVs) last month, up 94.5 per cent year on year, as it rewrote its sales record for a second consecutive month.
Zeekr, a premium EV unit...

items.title: PDD embraces China’s ‘city of the future’ after bruising regulatory clash and record fine
items.creator: Wency Chen
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: PDD embraces China’s ‘city of the future’ after bruising regulatory clash and record fine
items.content:
The operator of budget online marketplaces Temu and Pinduoduo is ramping up its commitment in a state-backed development zone – dubbed a “city of the future” in official discourse – months after the e-commerce group was hit by the heaviest penalty in a record food-safety crackdown and accused by regulators of obstructing enforcement.
PDD Holdings had more than 600 employees at its unit in the Xiongan New Area by the end of June, making it the development zone’s largest privately owned internet...

items.title: Lorenzi warns of economic conflict and unplottable solutions: ‘world without bearings’
items.creator: Xiaofei Xu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Lorenzi warns of economic conflict and unplottable solutions: ‘world without bearings’
items.content:
The global order has lost the structural anchors that once ensured stability, entering a volatile period of economic conflict with “no obvious solutions”, the head of a prominent French economic think tank has cautioned.
Reindustrialising “is far more difficult than it was 30 years ago”, said Jean-Herve Lorenzi, president of Le Cercle des economistes, which organises France’s flagship annual economic forum, Les Rencontres Economiques d’Aix-en-Provence. “If we had each defended our own countries’...

items.title: In the AI era, US-China competition hinges on who can adapt faster
items.creator: Marco Vicenzino
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: In the AI era, US-China competition hinges on who can adapt faster
items.content:
Power has historically been measured by indicators such as territory, population, industrial production, military capability and economic scale. Those foundations remain indispensable. But each technological revolution has changed the nature of geopolitical competition.
The Industrial Revolution rewarded manufacturing capacity. The 20th century rewarded industrial scale, military mobilisation and ideological reach. Globalisation rewarded efficiency, integration and financial connectivity. The...

items.title: Visa-free ‘dividends’: China drawing a boom in foreign visitors this summer
items.creator: June Xia,Ralph Jennings
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Visa-free ‘dividends’: China drawing a boom in foreign visitors this summer
items.content:
Nikolas Karageorge has mapped out a tour of China that starts in Shanghai and Beijing before heading deep inland – venturing well beyond the typical tourist trail.
“ It’s an ambitious itinerary, I know!” said the 37-year-old resident of Melbourne, Australia, about his path through Chengdu, Chongqing, Zhangjiajie, Guizhou, Guilin and finally Guangzhou. “I’ve heard China is very safe and easy to travel around so this is a great opportunity, as Australians currently have 30 days of visa-free...

items.title: As China tightens the fiscal net, it must strike a fine balance
items.creator: Michael Han
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: As China tightens the fiscal net, it must strike a fine balance
items.content:
The National Audit Office has accused Bank of China of dodging taxes. In its annual report, the audit office revealed that the state-owned commercial bank had used two of its subsidiaries to repackage 11 private equity funds as public investment vehicles, exploiting a regulatory tax exemption to evade 2.37 billion yuan (US$348.8 million) in taxes.
This is not an isolated case. It sits at the top of a much wider pattern. Look to the other end of the state enterprise hierarchy and you find...

items.title: Will US-China economic ties become self-reinforcing cycle of suspicion?
items.creator: David Willard
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Will US-China economic ties become self-reinforcing cycle of suspicion?
items.content:
In the ever-complex US-China economic relationship, the most consequential signals are not always those that command the biggest headlines. Tariffs, summits and disputes over semiconductors and other advanced technology attract understandable attention. However, a quieter change announced in Beijing on June 1 may prove more durable: China’s new State Council regulation on outbound investment, scheduled to take effect on July 1.
On its face, the new regulation appears purely administrative. It...

items.title: Why China’s billionaire-backed research universities are looking for more money
items.creator: He Huifeng
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Why China’s billionaire-backed research universities are looking for more money
items.content:
Five research-intensive universities in China that are heavily reliant on funding from philanthropic tycoons are expanding enrolment – doubling it in some cases – and adding hot tech majors.
But they are vying with hundreds of better-known, larger and longer-established universities – some of which are also admitting more students – as they seek more revenue from tuition fees.
Whether the newer universities’ unusual moneymaking models will be able to support their long-term research ambitions is...

items.title: China’s new investment law asserts control over offshore tech transfers in landmark move
items.creator: Ralph Jennings
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s new investment law asserts control over offshore tech transfers in landmark move
items.content:
China has enacted a sweeping overseas-investment law that aims to safeguard national interests against trade barriers and unauthorised use of advanced technology abroad, though analysts warn that the move could complicate operations for foreign partners.
The State Council’s 34-article Regulation on Overseas Investment, taking effect on Wednesday, authorises “necessary and defensive measures” to protect Chinese investors and interests overseas in response to foreign trade-related barriers.
Under...

items.title: Will slowing car sales in China reignite brutal price war in crowded market?
items.creator: Daniel Ren
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Will slowing car sales in China reignite brutal price war in crowded market?
items.content:
In the most bearish Chinese car market forecast by an international bank or consultancy, AlixPartners has predicted that deliveries will fall by 10 per cent this year on the back of a shaky economy and softening government support.
Weak buying interest was likely to fuel a brutal price war that would ensnare nearly all the country’s 100-odd carmakers, the global consultancy added.
It forecast that 24.6 million light vehicles would be delivered by Chinese carmakers this year, with 10 million of...

items.title: China challenges US, Japan’s grip on high-end carbon fibre with new T1100 production line
items.creator: Emma Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China challenges US, Japan’s grip on high-end carbon fibre with new T1100 production line
items.content:
China has stepped up mass production of ultra-high-strength carbon fibre amid export restrictions imposed by the United States and Japan – a move that could upend the global supply chain for a material critical to the aerospace, drone and robotics industries.
State-owned China National Building Material Group (CNBM) launched three production lines in east China’s Jiangsu province, including one facility over the weekend capable of producing 1,000 tonnes of high-strength T1100-grade carbon fibre...

items.title: China-Canada trade thaw falters as Beijing levies penalties for dumping pea starch
items.creator: Emma Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China-Canada trade thaw falters as Beijing levies penalties for dumping pea starch

images.alt: White pea starch powder is seen on a wooden spoon in a cast-iron pan. Photo: Shutterstock
images.title: White pea starch powder is seen on a wooden spoon in a cast-iron pan. Photo: Shutterstock

items.content:
Beijing has issued a preliminary verdict finding that pea-starch products imported from Canada were dumped in China, following Ottawa’s anti-dumping investigation into Chinese-made steel racks.
“Relevant authorities have made a preliminary determination that imported pea-starch products originating in Canada have been dumped,” the Ministry of Commerce said in a statement on Tuesday. “The dumping acts have caused material damage to the domestic pea-starch industry.”
Starting on Wednesday,...

items.title: China faces economic balancing act: AI and exports boom while consumers stay home
items.creator: Frank Chen,Xiaofei Xu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China faces economic balancing act: AI and exports boom while consumers stay home
items.content:
China’s leadership held two high-level meetings on Monday, focusing on solidifying new growth drivers such as artificial intelligence, decarbonisation and healthcare as it aims to bolster foreign trade while also calling for a more robust domestic market to boost consumption at home.
The meetings were the latest sign that Beijing aimed to sustain the AI and export boom while also addressing macroeconomic weak links, said Larry Hu, Macquarie Group’s chief China economist. The top leadership was...

items.title: EU seeks ‘tangible results’ on China trade deficit by October
items.creator: Neil Denslow,Raymond Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: EU seeks ‘tangible results’ on China trade deficit by October
items.content:
The European Union set an October deadline for “tangible results” in tackling its growing trade deficit with China, after the two sides held talks in Brussels in an attempt to avoid a trade war.
“Not everything will be solved, not everything will be fixed, but we think that between now and October, our teams have sufficient time to deliver the tangible results,” EU trade chief Maros Sefcovic told reporters on Monday, according to Bloomberg News. He spoke after “intensive, focused and...

items.title: How China’s new education plan tackles talent crunch, self-reliance amid tech challenges
items.creator: Huizhao Huang,Mandy Zuo
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: How China’s new education plan tackles talent crunch, self-reliance amid tech challenges
items.content:
China has placed serving national strategy and achieving self-reliance in technology and talent at the heart of its education agenda for the next five years, as policymakers respond to intensifying global technological competition amid a shrinking school-age population.
A State Council blueprint issued on Monday sets out 15 flagship programmes to build China into an education powerhouse, including strengthening vocational training, placing greater emphasis on science and engineering, and...

items.title: China’s factory activity returns to expansion in June but weak domestic demand lingers
items.creator: Alice Li
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s factory activity returns to expansion in June but weak domestic demand lingers
items.content:
China’s factory activity returned to expansionary territory in June, supported by strong exports, while other data signalled that domestic demand continued to show weakness.
The official manufacturing purchasing managers’ index (PMI) – an indicator of factory activity – rose to 50.3 in June from 50.0 in the previous month, according to data released on Tuesday by the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS).
The reading slightly beat the 50.03 projection by Chinese financial data provider Wind.
Using...

items.translated_title: Shanghai should step up as finance hub, as Hong Kong ‘not enough’, proposal says
items.content:
Shanghai has been urged to build itself into a hub serving the rising outbound investment needs of Chinese firms, potentially increasing rivalry with Hong Kong as both cities race to augment their status as financial centres.
The suggestion by Liu Xiaochun, vice-president of the Shanghai Finance Institute and a senior banker with three decades of experience, was made in mid-June at a closed-door meeting hosted by China Finance 40, a Beijing think tank comprising many top Chinese financial...
